metadata.author: Jeremiah
metadata.subject: God's Dealing with the Devastated Kingdom of Israel in the Economy of God under the Kingship of the Kings according to the Lusts and Passions of the Kings.

1: And Moab rebelled against Israel after the death of Ahab.

And Ahaziah fell through the lattice in his upper chamber which was in Samaria, and he became sick. And he sent messengers and said to them, Go, inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron, whether I will recover from this sickness.

But the Messenger of Jehovah said to Elijah the Tishbite, Arise, go up to meet the messengers of the king of Samaria, and say to them, Is it because there is no God in Israel that you are going to inquire of Baal-zebub, the god of Ekron?
2: And when Jehovah was about to take up Elijah by a whirlwind into heaven, Elijah and Elisha went from Gilgal.
And Elijah said to Elisha, Stay here, please; for Jehovah has sent me as far as Bethel. But Elisha said, As Jehovah lives and as your soul lives, I will not leave you. So they went down to Bethel.

3.text: Elijah represents the Old Testament prophets who were used by God to call the apostate kings and people back to the Law and the proper worship of Jehovah.

2.text: The journey from Gilgal to Bethel, then to Jericho, and finally across the Jordan represents the path of losing the old man and the natural strength to be prepared for the New Testament ministry.
